title: Lesson Quiz: Reflecting on Career Development
description: Assessment of the concept that Self-evaluation and growth.

questions.prompt: What does reflection help a student do?

choices.text: Identify growth, strengths, and areas for improvement
choices.feedback: Correct. Reflection helps students think carefully about progress, strengths, and next steps.
Reflection usually involves reviewing completed work and thinking about what it shows.
Strong reflection depends on evidence of progress and performance.
Reflection should include strengths and areas for improvement.

questions.prompt: A strong reflection should include specific evidence.

choices.feedback: Specific evidence makes reflection clearer and more useful.
Correct. Reflection is stronger when it points to clear evidence of growth or need.

questions.prompt: Which statement is the best example of reflection?

choices.text: I finished my work, so there is nothing to think about
choices.feedback: Reflection goes beyond completion and considers quality, growth, and next steps.

choices.text: I improved my organization because my portfolio now has clearer labels and categories
choices.feedback: This is a strong reflection statement, but the correct answer in this set is the version that includes both growth and future improvement.

choices.text: I improved my organization because my portfolio is clearer, and I still want to improve my speaking confidence
choices.feedback: Correct. This statement identifies a strength, gives evidence, and names an area for improvement.

choices.text: My work is done, so reflection is not necessary
choices.feedback: Reflection remains useful even after work is complete.

questions.prompt: Why is reflection useful for future planning?

choices.text: Because it removes the need to improve
choices.feedback: Reflection helps students improve; it does not remove that need.

choices.text: Because it helps students understand what to improve next
choices.feedback: This is a good idea, but the strongest answer in this set more fully explains how reflection supports future decisions.

choices.text: Because it guarantees every future choice will be easy
choices.feedback: Reflection can guide future choices, but it does not guarantee easy decisions.

choices.text: Because it helps students use what they learned to make better next-step decisions
choices.feedback: Correct. Reflection supports future planning by showing what has been learned and what should happen next.

questions.prompt: Reflection should only focus on mistakes and should not mention strengths.

choices.feedback: Strong reflection should include both strengths and areas for improvement.
Correct. Reflection is stronger when it recognizes progress as well as future needs.
